text: Definition of CKD: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is characterized by the presence of kidney damage or an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) of less than 60 mL/min/1.73 m², persisting for 3 months or more.
anchor_quote: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is characterized by the presence of kidney damage or an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) of less than 60 mL/min/1.73

text: CGA classification (cause + GFR + albuminuria): The 2012 Kidney Disease Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) CKD classification recommends specifying the cause of CKD and classifies the condition into 6 categories based on GFR (G1 to G5, with G3 split into 3a and 3b).
anchor_quote: The 2012 Kidney Disease Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) CKD classification recommends specifying the cause of CKD and classifies the condition into 6

text: GFR categories defining CKD stage 3: G3a: GFR 45 to 59 mL/min/1.73 m2; G3b: GFR 30 to 44 mL/min/1.73 m2.
Albuminuria categories (urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio): A1: ACR less than 30 mg/g; A2: ACR 30 to 299 mg/g; A3: ACR greater than 300 mg/g.

Repeat testing to distinguish AKI from CKD: Kidney function tests should be repeated within 2 weeks of the initial finding of decreased eGFR for patients in whom the distinction between AKI and CKD is unclear.
anchor_quote: Kidney function tests should be repeated within 2 weeks of the initial finding of decreased eGFR for patients in whom the distinction between AKI and CKD is

text: Hemoglobin monitoring cadence by stage: Hemoglobin levels should be checked annually in stage 3 CKD, every 6 months in stages 4 and 5, and monthly in dialysis patients.
anchor_quote: Hemoglobin levels should be checked annually in stage 3 CKD, every 6 months in stages 4 and 5, and monthly in dialysis patients

text: Nephrology referral criteria: Patients should be referred to a nephrologist at any stage of CKD or for urinary abnormalities, especially when the eGFR falls below 60 mL/min/1.73m2.
anchor_quote: Patients should be referred to a nephrologist at any stage of CKD or for urinary abnormalities, especially when the eGFR falls below 60 mL/min/1.73m
